steps.title: Define what the blank must mean
steps.content: The corpus is used to test hypotheses about "how ______ a word is in spoken and written English." The example that follows — proving that "world" is the eighth most commonly used noun — is about frequency of occurrence. The blank must describe how widespread or frequently a word appears in the language.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — pervasive: Means present or occurring throughout; widespread. "How pervasive a word is" directly expresses how frequently it appears across the language. ✓
  • Choice B — credible: Means believable or trustworthy. Words are not evaluated for credibility; the passage is about occurrence counts, not reliability. ✗
  • Choice C — assertive: Means confident or forceful in manner. This describes personality, not how often something appears. ✗
  • Choice D — profound: Means having deep meaning or insight. The passage concerns raw frequency data, not the depth or significance of words. ✗
That matches Choice A — pervasive.
